text.title: Saga Pattern for Distributed Transactions Examples
text.summary: Worked example: place-order saga (order → billing → inventory → shipping) using Temporal orchestration.

example.context: Order service kicks off place-order; billing charges card; inventory reserves stock; shipping schedules pickup. Any failure → compensate.
example.walk: Step 1: steps = [order.create-pending → order.cancel-order, billing.charge → billing.refund-card, inventory.reserve → inventory.release-stock, shipping.schedule-pickup → shipping.cancel-pickup]. All idempotent via saga_id+step_id key. Step 2: 4 steps + cross-team → orchestration; pick Temporal. Step 3: each service has outbox table; Debezium publishes to Kafka. Step 4: Temporal workflow PlaceOrderSaga: ExecuteActivity(CreatePendingOrder) → ExecuteActivity(ChargeCard) → ExecuteActivity(ReserveStock) → ExecuteActivity(SchedulePickup); on Activity error → reverse-order compensations. Step 5: chaos-test: kill inventory mid-saga → Temporal triggers RefundCard + CancelOrder (skip SchedulePickup since not executed yet). All idempotent retries verified.
example.output: Cross-service consistency guaranteed (compensated on failure); operator sees one Temporal workflow execution per saga; manual reconciliation goes from 30/week to 0.
